# Third-party fixture licenses — share-alike obligations

This file enumerates fixtures in this directory that are **derived from
third-party works under a copyleft / share-alike license**, and records
the attribution + share-alike notices that license requires us to
propagate downstream.

The per-file provenance manifest lives in [`README.md`](README.md); this
file is the dedicated CC-BY-SA-4.0 attribution block that the share-alike
clause asks for. Where both files apply, this file is the source of
truth for licence terms; the README row is the short summary that points
back here.

## CC-BY-SA-4.0 fixtures

The fixtures listed below contain prose excerpted from English
Wikipedia. Wikipedia article text is published under the
[Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International Public
License (CC-BY-SA-4.0)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/legalcode).
That license requires that any redistribution of the work — including
modified or derivative versions — preserve attribution, indicate
modifications, and remain under the same CC-BY-SA-4.0 license.

### Affected files

| File | SHA-256 |
|------|---------|
| `states.csv`    | `7b1a2c024dbc457e213e8b42b74a81b8109fbefb3fefeb8a4f1fca71e3827e71` |
| `states.json`   | `121624ecbf36d83bdbf905e2a379a9350018b856aaef418ff02e2599f6726be2` |
| `states.xlsx`   | `3a4748fb80ebf1e381cfbfe979368132025ddf4823a3f68a73b9485c1f6edcbc` |
| `states.sqlite` | `7d7166006612736e9ae12c89cf05f78932bf70b33c819a44725505245ab55dc3` |

All four files are alternate serializations of the same 50-row US
states reference table. The CC-BY-SA-4.0 obligation attaches to the
prose carried in the `description` and `motto` columns; the remaining
columns (`name`, `capital`, `admitted`, `order`, `population`,
`latitude`, `longitude`, `midlat`, `midlon`) are US federal facts in
the public domain under 17 USC §105 and carry no share-alike
obligation of their own.

### Attribution

- **Author** — Wikipedia contributors.
- **Title** — Individual state articles on the English Wikipedia
  ("Washington (state)", "Oregon", "Arkansas", "Montana", and 46
  others, one per row).
- **Source** — `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/<State_name>`
  (per-row lead-paragraph excerpts; the per-row `name` column gives
  the article title). The aggregator index is
  [`List of states and territories of the United States`](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_states_and_territories_of_the_United_States).
- **License** — `CC-BY-SA-4.0` (SPDX identifier). Full text at
  <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/legalcode>.
- **Retrieved on** — Excerpted into the predecessor `kelvin-modules`
  fixture tree on or before 2025-05-10 (kelvin-tabular initial commit
  `e8b7e1d`). Vendored unchanged into kaos-tabular on 2026-04-03
  (first KAOS commit `aef1072`); the SQLite/XLSX shards followed on
  the same day via commit `e03b9b5`. The `.json` shard is a
  re-serialization of `states.csv` generated by 273V on the same
  day and carries the same CC-BY-SA-4.0 obligation on the prose
  columns.

### Indication of changes

The Wikipedia lead paragraphs were excerpted (not edited) into per-row
cells of a tabular fixture. Visible artifacts of the excerpting
process — pronunciation IPA brackets (e.g. `i / ˈ w ɒ ʃ ɪ ŋ t ən /`),
footnote markers (e.g. `[ 1 ]`, `[ 6 ]`), and section-link spacing —
are preserved verbatim from the upstream and are *not* 273V
modifications. The `motto` column contains the article's motto
inline rendering, again excerpted verbatim. No prose was rewritten,
paraphrased, summarized, or augmented by 273V.

### Share-alike notice

Any redistribution of these fixtures — modified or unmodified, in
whole or in part — must:

1. Carry attribution to the Wikipedia contributors of the relevant
   state article(s) (per the URL pattern above).
2. Indicate any further modifications relative to the upstream
   Wikipedia text.
3. Be licensed under `CC-BY-SA-4.0` (or a later compatible version
   per CC-BY-SA-4.0 §4(b)).

The fixtures are bundled only with the kaos-tabular test suite and
are **not** included in the published `kaos-tabular` wheel; the
share-alike obligation therefore does not propagate to library
consumers. If a future release packages any of these files inside
a published artifact, that artifact must reproduce this attribution
block alongside the file.

## Non-CC-BY-SA-4.0 fixtures

All other fixtures in this directory (LEDES98B specification sample,
the three hand-crafted micro-fixtures) carry no share-alike
obligation. Their license terms are documented per-row in
[`README.md`](README.md).
